meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve Debugging Roadblocks
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing strategies to overcome persistent debugging issues. A technical case study for students using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Debugging paralysis often stems from poorly architected SaaS pricing models that create technical debt. By integrating automated pricing logic with Claude Code, you decouple business rules from your core application logic, allowing for cleaner codebases, easier testing, and a scalable infrastructure that keeps your development momentum high.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen filled with red errors, the silence of your IDE echoing the frustration of a project that feels like it is falling apart. You’ve built the features, but the logic governing your SaaS pricing is tangled in your core application code, creating a debugging nightmare where every small change breaks the entire system.

This isn't a failure of your ability; it's a failure of architectural separation. By treating your pricing model as an independent service rather than an afterthought, you reclaim control. Let's transform your development workflow from a chaotic cycle of trial-and-error into a precision-engineered process using modern AI-driven tools.

steps.title: Decouple Pricing Logic
steps.description: Extract pricing rules from your main codebase into a standalone module to prevent spaghetti code and minimize side effects during debugging.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my current pricing logic into a modular, stateless service. Extract all hardcoded constants into a configuration schema.

steps.title: Implement Pricing Middleware
steps.description: Create a robust middleware layer to handle billing calculations, ensuring that debugging is isolated to one specific point in the request lifecycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a middleware pattern for pricing validation that logs every input and output for auditing and debugging purposes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'create pricing validation middleware with detailed logging'

steps.title: Automate Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Use AI to generate comprehensive test suites for your pricing logic, ensuring edge cases like trial expirations are covered.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a unit test suite for my pricing middleware covering edge cases, boundary values, and error states.

steps.title: Integrate Observability Hooks
steps.description: Inject telemetry into your pricing service to visualize data flow, making it easier to pinpoint exactly where calculations fail.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add OpenTelemetry instrumentation to my pricing service to trace requests through the billing flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'instrument pricing service with OpenTelemetry traces'

steps.title: Continuous Feedback Loop
steps.description: Establish a CI/CD pipeline that runs your pricing tests against every commit to catch regressions before they reach production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that executes my pricing test suite on every pull request.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate github actions pipeline for pricing unit tests'

pro_tips: Always use a decimal-based library (like Dinero.js) for money; never use floating-point math to avoid rounding errors during debugging.
Map your pricing states to a finite state machine; it makes debugging logic transitions significantly faster.
Implement 'Dry Run' modes in your pricing API that allow you to simulate billing without hitting the production database.
Use Claude Code to document your 'Why' behind every pricing decision directly in the code comments for future-proof maintenance.
Isolate external SDKs (like Stripe) behind an abstraction layer so you can mock them entirely during your local debugging sessions.
content.next_steps: After completing this integration, deploy your pricing service to a staging environment and perform load testing using your new observability hooks to ensure performance stability.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build scalable SaaS products without the debugging headache. Take your newfound skills and apply them to your portfolio to stand out to potential employers. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
